ksa_text: Knowledge of lawful bases for data processing under applicable regulations, including consent requirements, contractual necessity, legal obligations, vital interests, public task authority, and legitimate interest balancing tests.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of functionality, quality, and security requirements and how these will apply to specific items of supply (i.e., elements and processes).

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of functionality, quality, and security requirements and how these will apply to specific items of supply (i.e., elements and processes).

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge case — included on balance: knowledge contains 0 RSK-relevant signal(s) (domain context) within Risk & Compliance (domain affinity 0.90). STRM composite is strong (0.4552). A practicing Data Compliance Analyst would encounter this competency regularly.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in RSK job postings or cert curricula (CRISC, FRM, NIST AI RMF, CISM). Would a working Data Compliance Analyst self-report this as required?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the competency is implicit in Data Compliance Analyst work (domain affinity 0.90, 0 RSK signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching systematically underrepresents risk management and governance KSAs.
